item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-027","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":false,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer correctly rejects fully handing pre-merge review to AI and separates flow/automation checks from human judgment. It explicitly assigns AI/CI to mechanical, high-coverage checks such as lint, type, tests, static scanning, common bugs, security/performance hints, and diff risk surfacing, while preserving human responsibility for product intent, business rules, architecture direction, risk acceptance, and merge accountability. It avoids the fail signal of merely saying 'AI assists, human leads' by giving concrete review boundaries and evidence gates."}
